_comment: Canonical default content for Vintage 101 Marketplace. This file is baked into the static pages (works with no JS) AND used to seed Netlify Blobs on first run. The admin backend edits a draft copy of this shape; Publish promotes draft to published; public pages hydrate from published. Editable list items carry an 'id' so they can be added, removed, and reordered. No em dashes or en dashes anywhere in copy.

site.tagline: A marketplace of the good old stuff.
site.blurb: A family run vintage marketplace on the Coos Bay boardwalk. More than twenty independent vendors of antiques, records, furniture, and one of a kind finds.
site.address: 270 S Broadway Ave, Coos Bay, OR 97420

hero.title: A marketplace of the good old stuff.
hero.lead: Vintage 101 is a family run marketplace with more than twenty vendors, each keeping their own booth. Antiques, mid-century furniture, glassware, records, gems and minerals, vintage clothing, gourmet chocolate, and a constantly changing trove of finds. Something new turns up on every visit.
hero.alt: Open doorway of Vintage 101 showing racks and shelves of antique and vintage goods

marketplace.title: A booth around every corner
marketplace.lead: Each vendor keeps their own little world, from cottage and shabby chic to glassware, Americana, Southwestern collectibles, and racks of vintage clothing. Wander slow. The fun is in the find.

cards.title: Cottage corners
cards.alt: Cottage style booth with an apron dress on a mannequin, owl decor, and a bar cart

cards.title: The Welcome booth
cards.alt: Booth with blue flowers, owl figurines on a teal etagere, and Southwestern blankets

cards.title: Color by the shelf
cards.alt: Lit display case full of amber, green, and vintage glassware

cards.title: The dish hutch
cards.body: Pyrex, pitchers, and pottery by the shelf.
cards.alt: White hutch filled with vintage pitchers, Pyrex, dishes, mugs, and canisters

cards.title: Fiesta and color
cards.alt: Table stacked with colorful Fiesta ware plates, bowls, and pitchers

cards.title: Roosters and crocks
cards.alt: Country hutch with ceramic roosters, copper molds, crocks, and canisters

cards.title: Racks to rummage
cards.body: Dresses and one of a kind pieces.

cards.title: Statement dresses

cards.title: Leather and shearling
cards.alt: Tan shearling coat on a mannequin with a cowboy hat

cards.title: Around the world
cards.alt: Oak cabinet topped with a vintage world globe and filled with Southwestern pottery and collectibles

records.title: The coast's crate-digging stop
records.body: Inside Vintage 101 you will find Wax Museum Records and MCM Vintage: alphabetized flip bins of vinyl, framed album art, music posters at three for fifty, and a wall of original show flyers. From Pet Sounds to Kraftwerk, new arrivals land all the time.

images.alt: Wall of vinyl records in flip bins with framed album covers and local show flyers above
Framed album covers including Madonna, the Monkees, and Pet Sounds beside a posters sign
The Wax Museum Records and MCM Vintage corner with framed movie posters, bins of vinyl, and cassette racks

chocolate.title: Handmade in Oregon
chocolate.body: A full glass case of truffles, creams, clusters, and barks, all handmade in Oregon. Milk and dark, most pieces just a dollar or two. The sweetest reason to slow down between the booths.
chocolate.alt: Glass display case of gourmet chocolate truffles and clusters labeled handmade in Oregon

furniture.title: Furniture 101, right next door
furniture.lead: Step into the furniture department next door for antique, vintage, mid-century, and newer pieces, lighting, and art. From a carved Edwardian sideboard to clean mid-century lines, the larger pieces live here with room to breathe.

cards.title: The furniture department
cards.body: Antique to mid-century, with room to roam.
cards.alt: Wide view of the Vintage 101 furniture department with chairs, dressers, tables, and rugs

art.lead: Original paintings from local artists, framed posters, a dated archive of LIFE magazines, gems and minerals, sterling and Firefly jewelry, militaria, and Americana. The cases reward the curious.

vendors.intro: Vintage 101 is home to more than twenty independent vendors across the marketplace. Wax Museum Records and MCM Vintage are two you will find by name. The booths below show the kinds of finds each corner of the shop keeps, and the lineup changes as vendors come and go.

items.name: Wax Museum Records
items.description: The coast's crate-digging stop. Alphabetized flip bins of vinyl, framed album art, and a wall of original show flyers. New arrivals land all the time.
items.alt: Wall of vinyl records in flip bins with framed album covers and local show flyers above

items.name: MCM Vintage
items.description: A mid-century corner of the record room, with classic covers and posters worth flipping through.
items.alt: Framed album covers including Madonna, the Monkees, and Pet Sounds beside a posters sign

items.name: Handmade Oregon Chocolate
items.description: A full glass case of truffles, creams, clusters, and barks, all handmade in Oregon. Milk and dark, most pieces just a dollar or two.
items.alt: Glass display case of gourmet chocolate truffles and clusters labeled handmade in Oregon

items.alt: Framed movie and music posters hanging from the ceiling including Goldfinger

about.title: A family marketplace on the boardwalk
about.lead: Vintage 101 started with a simple idea: give good old things a second life, and a place to be found.
body: We are a family run marketplace at the south end of the Coos Bay Boardwalk. More than twenty independent dealers share the space, and each one keeps their own corner, from cottage and shabby chic to glassware, records, and Southwestern collectibles.

Wander the booths and cases, then step next door to Furniture 101 for the larger pieces, from a carved Edwardian sideboard to clean mid-century lines. Wax Museum Records sits inside for the crate diggers, and there is a case of chocolate handmade in Oregon for when you need a reason to slow down.
Nothing here stays the same for long. Dealers restock, trade, and rearrange, so there is always something new to find. Come in, take your time, and see what turns up.
about.alt: Wall of vinyl records in flip bins with framed album covers and local show flyers above

visit.title: Find us on South Broadway
visit.lead: Vintage 101 sits at the south end of the Coos Bay Boardwalk on South Broadway. Easy parking, a marketplace to wander, and the furniture department right next door. Open daily, 11:00 am to 5:00 pm.
visit.note: Looking for records? Wax Museum Records lives inside Vintage 101.
